xposts.title: Visual system: generative model punished by sensory mismatch
xposts.excerpt: The visual system is a generative model that gets punished when it contradicts sensory patterns. In the absence of sensory patterns, it generates freely: during dreams and out of body experiences, it invents hallucinations.

xposts.title: Perception constrains space of possible worlds, not access
xposts.excerpt: Perception is not giving us access to the world, it constrains the space of possible worlds. Similarly, the data that an LLM is trained on constrains the space of possible universes. With enough data, LLMs form integrated world models.

xposts.title: Dream body as unconditional control interface
xposts.excerpt: The AI may not have a body, just as you don’t have a body while you dream. The body is the interface that you experience yourself to be unconditionally and immediately in control of when you act in the environment.

xposts.title: Dream perspectivity without self or mine-ness
xposts.excerpt: Perspectivity and mine-ness are specific modes and experiences of consciousness: they are dependent on consciousness but not vice versa: there can be awareness of the contents of a dream scene while no self is present and no perspective is being taken
